title: dalea eriophylla var. frankenioides
intro: Dalea eriophylla var. frankenioides, also known as the White-Flower Prickly-pear, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Dalea eriophylla var. frankenioides include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, tinctures, and salves for topical application, and are typically used to treat various skin conditions and minor injuries.
